title: aspidium filix-mas var. normale
intro: Aspidium filix-mas var. normale, also known as the male fern, is a type of vascular plant belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating parasitic worm infections, such as tapeworms and roundworms, due to its anthelmintic properties. The health benefits of Aspidium filix-mas var. normale include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve the use of its rhizomes, which are dried and processed to create teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal purposes.
